handoff_goal: Plan Settings > Providers import/export for shareable provider configuration bundles, including default secret-free export, optional high-friction secret export, side-effect-free import preview, explicit conflict handling, and no active/default provider transfer.

current_project_roles.evidence_source: User confirmation and current working directory.
current_project_roles.notes: Current repository is also the implementation target.

current_project_roles.scope: Desktop Settings > Providers UI, provider API/store/types, server provider REST API/service/types, provider persistence, and managed settings sync.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: Discussion handoff and targeted live repository reads.

target_project_roles.scope: Add provider import/export capability for the current app's Settings provider surface.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: User request, specification artifacts, and live repository reads.
target_project_roles.notes: Sharing is provider-record focused, not local active state transfer.

required_target_paths_to_verify: Exact i18n keys and location for Settings provider dialog text.
Whether import/export UI logic should be extracted from Settings.tsx into provider-specific components.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-001 Add Settings > Providers import/export for shareable provider configuration.
MP-002 Import/export covers saved provider records only.
MP-003 Users may choose whether to export API keys/secrets.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Secret export is omitted or made default.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-004 Secret-free export is the default.
MP-005 Secret-including export requires a separate dangerous action, warning, second confirmation, no remembered choice, and labeling.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Secret export is remembered, casual, or unlabeled.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-006 Import conflicts default to add/rename; overwrite requires explicit selection.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Import silently overwrites an existing provider.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-007 Active/default provider selection is not exported or imported.
MP-008 Import uses preview before commit with diagnostics.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Import writes during preview or lacks diagnostics.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-009 Workflow import/export dialog is a process precedent, not provider schema source.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Workflow template semantics leak into provider bundle design.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-010 Resolve raw-vs-masked provider API key behavior before claiming export security.
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Export security relies only on frontend masking assumptions.

must_preserve: Provider import/export covers saved provider records only.
Users may choose secret-including export only through high-friction dangerous flow.
Secret export choice is not remembered.
Credential-bearing export artifacts are clearly labeled.
Import preview occurs before commit and is side-effect free.
Import conflicts default to add/rename; overwrite is explicit.
Active/default provider selection is not exported or imported.
Import does not auto-activate or write active provider env.
New imports use generated local IDs by default.
Official OAuth/login state is not exported.

known_risks: Raw-vs-masked provider API key behavior must be resolved during implementation design before security-ready export claims.
